transcript: m瑟s 冒蓹 瑟n藞t蓻nt
definitions: To fail to understand or grasp the true purpose, aim, or meaning behind an action, statement, or plan.
To misinterpret the underlying objective or goal.
examples: He completely missed the intent of her sarcastic remark.
The new policy missed the intent of the original legislation.
Don't miss the intent behind their generous offer.
